name: MLAgentBench
aliases: MLAgentBench: Evaluating Language Agents on Machine Learning Experimentation
current_status: Active public benchmark with an arXiv paper and open-source repository.
positioning: Benchmark for end-to-end machine learning experimentation by language agents.
boundary_notes: Included because it evaluates autonomous ML experimentation across data, modeling, training, and analysis loops. It is broader than a generic coding benchmark because the goal is to improve ML models through iterative experimentation.
execution_environment: Interactive task environments that resemble human research workflows. The repository supports local execution and Docker, and tasks may require dataset preparation and repeated experiment runs.
human_roles: Benchmark creators define tasks and starter code. Users configure dependencies, provide API keys, accept competition rules where required, run agents, and review experimental outputs.
autonomy_rationale: MLAgentBench targets agents that autonomously run ML experiments and improve models, but within bounded tasks, starter code, user-provided infrastructure, and explicit evaluation scripts.
iteration_loop: Iterative ML experimentation is central to the benchmark: agents read and write files, run experiments, inspect outputs, and revise approaches over multiple steps.
stopping_and_guardrails: The repository recommends Docker because agents modify and execute files. Tasks require explicit environment setup, API credentials, and Kaggle authentication for some datasets.
known_failure_modes: poor transfer to recent Kaggle challenges
benchmarks_used: MLAgentBench itself across 13 ML engineering tasks.
validation_strength: Benchmarked with public tasks, a public paper, and repository-reported quantitative results.
evaluation_limitations: Performance varies widely across tasks, from strong results on older datasets to 0% on some recent Kaggle challenges, so benchmark scores depend heavily on task recency and environment setup.
dataset_url: [uncertain] No standalone dataset page was identified; the benchmark spans 13 tasks and prepares task data through the official repository.
leaderboard_url: [uncertain] No standalone official leaderboard was identified; results are reported in the paper and repository.
source_quality: Primary sources: official arXiv paper and official Stanford SNAP repository.
predecessors: [uncertain] No explicit predecessor benchmark is named in the consulted primary sources.
evaluated_by: [uncertain] No separate external benchmark evaluating MLAgentBench itself was identified in the consulted sources.
milestones: Public repository released with setup instructions, Docker guidance, and benchmark scripts.
version_history: the public repository continues to host setup, task preparation, and evaluation workflows
adoption_signals: Public log repository is linked from the official repository
supersession_notes: Not superseded in the consulted sources. Later related benchmarks such as MLE-bench appear adjacent rather than clearly replacing it [uncertain].
real_world_applications: studying iterative experiment planning and execution
comparing language models on ML engineering tasks
deployment_constraints: Requires Python 3.10, benchmark dependencies, code-execution permissions, possible Docker usage, Kaggle authentication for some tasks, and API keys for supported models.
cost_and_runtime: Cost and runtime depend on the number of experiment runs, chosen model APIs, dataset preparation, and available compute; the repository does not publish a single fixed budget [uncertain].
scientific_integrity_risks: misleading comparisons when environment setup differs
overgeneralizing from 13 tasks to all ML research
security_and_safety_risks: external data downloads and Kaggle access increase operational risk
API-key handling and container isolation must be managed carefully
mitigations: public logs and scripts for inspection
policy_relevance: Relevant to evaluation of autonomous experimentation systems, reproducibility of agent benchmarks, and governance of code-executing research agents.
